title: Temperature Sensor Short Error
shortDescription: The dryer's temperature sensor (thermistor) has a short circuit, sending abnormally low resistance readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code tS indicates the thermistor inside the dryer drum has shorted, causing its resistance to drop to near zero ohms. The control board interprets this as an extremely high temperature condition and shuts down the dryer for safety. The thermistor is a small sensor mounted on the blower housing or inside the exhaust duct that monitors air temperature during drying cycles. A shorted thermistor sends inaccurate data, preventing the dryer from regulating heat properly.

causes.cause: Thermistor internal short circuit due to age or heat damage
Pinched or damaged thermistor wiring creating a short
Moisture or condensation on the thermistor connector causing a short
Incorrect replacement thermistor installed (wrong resistance value)

symptoms: Error code tS displayed on the control panel
Dryer shuts down immediately or refuses to start
Dryer runs but produces no heat
Dryer overheats before the error appears

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer or turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and attempt to run a timed dry cycle. If the error was caused by a temporary glitch, the reset may clear it.

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Access the thermistor by removing the lower front panel or the back panel depending on your model. The thermistor is typically a small oval-shaped sensor mounted on the blower housing or exhaust duct with two wires. Check for obvious damage, discoloration, or melting.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wire harness. Set your multimeter to ohms and measure resistance across the two terminals. At room temperature (about 77 degrees F), a Samsung dryer thermistor should read approximately 10,000 ohms (10 kohms). A reading near 0 ohms confirms a short circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the single mounting screw or clip holding the thermistor in place. Disconnect the old thermistor and install the new one. Secure it with the mounting hardware and reconnect the wire harness. Reassemble the dryer panel.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the thermistor does not clear the tS error, the control board's temperature sensing circuit may be damaged. A technician can test the board and wiring harness for faults.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer tS error thermistor replacement

faq.q: What is the difference between tS and tO errors?
faq.a: The tS error indicates the thermistor has a short circuit (resistance too low), while tO indicates an open circuit (resistance infinitely high). Both point to a faulty thermistor but represent opposite failure modes.

faq.q: Can I test the thermistor without removing it?
faq.a: You can test it in place by disconnecting the wire harness and measuring resistance at the connector. However, removing it gives you better access and lets you visually inspect for damage.

faq.q: How long does a Samsung dryer thermistor last?
faq.a: Thermistors typically last 10-15 years, but exposure to excessive heat, lint buildup, and moisture can shorten their lifespan. Regular vent cleaning helps prolong sensor life.

title: Temperature Sensor Open Circuit Error
shortDescription: The dryer's temperature sensor (thermistor) has an open circuit, meaning it is no longer conducting and cannot report temperature.
fullDescription: Error code tO indicates the thermistor circuit is open, meaning the sensor wire is broken internally or the connection has been severed. The control board reads infinite resistance, which it interprets as a sensor failure. Without temperature feedback, the dryer cannot safely regulate heat output and will shut down or refuse to start. This is the counterpart to the tS error, which indicates a short circuit rather than an open circuit.

causes.cause: Thermistor internal wire break due to thermal fatigue
Disconnected or loose thermistor wire connector
Broken wire in the harness between thermistor and control board
Corroded connector pins creating an open circuit

symptoms: Error code tO on the display panel
Dryer will not start or shuts off immediately
No heat is produced during the drying cycle
Auto-dry cycles cannot sense temperature and run indefinitely or stop prematurely

fixSteps.title: Check the thermistor wire connection
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Access the thermistor location (blower housing area). Check that the wire connector is firmly attached to the thermistor. Reconnect if loose. Also inspect for corrosion on the pins and clean with contact cleaner if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor for continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor connector. Set your multimeter to the ohms scale. Place probes on the thermistor terminals. At room temperature, expect approximately 10,000 ohms. An OL (over limit) or infinite reading confirms an open circuit and the thermistor must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the wiring harness
fixSteps.instruction: If the thermistor tests good but the error persists, trace the wiring from the thermistor to the control board. Look for broken, pinched, or burned wires. Test continuity through each wire. Repair or replace damaged sections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw or clip holding the thermistor. Pull the old sensor off and install the new one in the same position. Secure it and reconnect the wire harness. Reassemble the panel and test.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor and wiring both test good but the error persists, the control board input circuit may be damaged and requires professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer tO error temperature sensor open circuit fix

faq.q: Can a loose wire cause the tO error?
faq.a: Yes. A loose or disconnected wire is one of the most common causes of the tO error. The open circuit could simply be a connection issue rather than a failed sensor. Always check the connector first.

faq.q: Is the thermistor expensive to replace?
faq.a: No. Samsung dryer thermistors are one of the most affordable parts, typically costing between $8 and $20. The repair is also relatively straightforward, making it a good DIY project.

faq.q: What happens if I run the dryer with a faulty thermistor?
faq.a: The dryer will either refuse to start or will not regulate heat properly. Running without accurate temperature sensing could cause the dryer to overheat or not heat at all, potentially damaging clothes or creating a fire hazard.

title: General Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The dryer's temperature sensing system has detected an abnormality, either a short or open circuit in the thermistor.
fullDescription: Error code tE is a general temperature sensor error that may appear on Samsung dryers when the thermistor is not providing expected readings. On some models, tE is the generic code, while tS (short) and tO (open) provide more specific diagnosis. The tE code means the control board cannot accurately read the drum or exhaust air temperature. This could be due to a failed thermistor, wiring issue, or in rare cases, a control board fault. The dryer will not operate safely without accurate temperature sensing.

causes.cause: Failed thermistor (open or short circuit)
Loose thermistor wire connector
Damaged wiring between thermistor and control board
Control board temperature input circuit failure

symptoms: Error code tE on the control panel display
Dryer refuses to start or stops mid-cycle
No heat or excessive heat during drying
Sensor dry cycles do not function properly

fixSteps.title: Power reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dryer from power for 5 minutes. Reconnect and try starting a Timed Dry cycle. If the error was transient, the reset may clear it.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the thermistor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Remove the lower front panel or back panel to access the thermistor. Verify the wire connector is securely attached. Clean any corrosion from the pins with electrical contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure its resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (77 degrees F), expect approximately 10,000 ohms. A reading of 0 ohms indicates a short, and OL indicates an open circuit. Either condition requires replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unscrew the mounting hardware and remove the old thermistor. Install the replacement in the same position, secure it, and reconnect the wire harness. Test the dryer on a short timed dry cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor has been replaced and the wiring is intact but the tE error persists, the main control board's temperature sensing circuit may need professional testing and replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer tE error code thermistor fix

faq.q: Is the tE error the same as tS and tO?
faq.a: The tE code is a general temperature error. On newer Samsung dryers, the code is further differentiated: tS for a short circuit and tO for an open circuit. On older models, tE covers both conditions.

faq.q: Can lint buildup cause the tE error?
faq.a: Not directly, but severe lint buildup can cause the dryer to overheat, which can damage the thermistor over time, eventually leading to the tE error. Regular lint and vent cleaning extends sensor life.

faq.q: Where exactly is the thermistor located?
faq.a: On most Samsung dryers, the thermistor is mounted on the blower housing or inside the exhaust duct at the rear of the drum. On some front-load models, it is accessible by removing the lower front panel. Check your model's service manual for the exact location.

title: Main Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The primary temperature sensor (main thermistor) has failed, preventing the dryer from monitoring drum air temperature.
fullDescription: Error code tE1 specifically identifies a failure in the main thermistor, which is the primary temperature sensor used to monitor the exhaust air temperature in the dryer. Samsung dryers with multiple temperature sensors use numbered tE codes to distinguish which sensor has failed. The tE1 code points to the main thermistor, usually located on the blower housing. This sensor is critical for all drying modes, and its failure will prevent any cycle from running properly.

causes.cause: Main thermistor failure due to age or overheating
Disconnected main thermistor connector
Wire damage in the main thermistor circuit
Control board input failure for the main thermistor channel

symptoms: Error code tE1 displayed on the panel
Dryer will not start any cycle
Dryer stops shortly after starting
No heat produced or inconsistent heating

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. Reconnect power and attempt to start a cycle. Note whether the error returns immediately or after a period of operation.

fixSteps.title: Check the main thermistor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and access the blower housing area. Locate the main thermistor and verify its connector is secure. Inspect for corrosion, melted plastic, or loose pins.

fixSteps.title: Test the main thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature, expect about 10,000 ohms. Values near 0 (short) or infinite/OL (open) indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the main thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty thermistor by unscrewing the mounting hardware. Install the new thermistor in the same location, reconnect the wiring, and reassemble the access panel.

whenToCallPro: If the new thermistor does not resolve tE1, the control board or wiring harness should be professionally diagnosed.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer tE1 main thermistor error fix

faq.q: How do I know which thermistor is the main one?
faq.a: The main thermistor (tE1) is typically located on the blower housing at the back of the dryer drum. It is the primary sensor used for all cycle types. Secondary sensors, if present, are located elsewhere in the duct system.

faq.q: Can I use a generic thermistor?
faq.a: It is recommended to use a Samsung OEM thermistor or a compatible replacement with the same resistance specification (10 kohms at 77F). A generic thermistor with different characteristics may cause inaccurate temperature readings.

faq.q: Does the tE1 error indicate a fire risk?
faq.a: The error itself is a safety feature that prevents the dryer from operating without temperature monitoring. However, if the thermistor failed due to overheating (from a clogged vent), the underlying cause should be addressed to prevent fire risk.

title: Secondary Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The secondary temperature sensor has malfunctioned, providing inaccurate readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code tE2 identifies a failure in the secondary thermistor used in Samsung dryers that have dual temperature sensors. The secondary thermistor may be located in the duct area or on the heater housing, depending on the model. It provides additional temperature data to help the control board regulate heat more precisely and detect overheating conditions. When this sensor fails, the dryer loses part of its temperature monitoring capability, which may affect sensor dry accuracy and safety.

causes.cause: Secondary thermistor failure (open or short)
Loose wiring connector at the secondary sensor
Heat damage to the secondary sensor due to restricted airflow
Control board secondary channel input fault

symptoms: Error code tE2 on the display
Dryer may still run but sensor dry does not work correctly
Clothes come out still damp or over-dried
Dryer shuts off prematurely on auto-dry cycles

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes and restore power. Run a timed dry cycle to check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect the secondary thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and remove the appropriate panel. The secondary thermistor is typically near the heater assembly or in the exhaust duct. Check its connector for secure attachment and any signs of heat damage.

fixSteps.title: Test the secondary thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test with a multimeter. Room temperature reading should be approximately 10,000 ohms. Readings significantly outside this range indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the secondary thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor and install the replacement. Ensure the new sensor is mounted securely in the same position and the wire connector is firmly attached.

whenToCallPro: If both thermistors test good and the tE2 error persists, the control board input circuit needs professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer tE2 secondary thermistor error

faq.q: Do all Samsung dryers have a secondary thermistor?
faq.a: No. Entry-level models may only have a single thermistor. Mid-range and premium models with advanced sensor dry features typically have two or more temperature sensors.

faq.q: Can a clogged vent damage the secondary thermistor?
faq.a: Yes. A clogged exhaust vent causes hot air to recirculate inside the dryer, exposing temperature sensors to higher-than-normal heat. This accelerated thermal stress can cause premature sensor failure.

faq.q: Is the tE2 error as critical as tE1?
faq.a: The tE2 error is important but may be less critical than tE1 since the primary sensor (tE1) handles the main temperature regulation. However, the secondary sensor provides safety monitoring and sensor dry accuracy, so it should still be repaired promptly.

title: Inlet Thermistor Error
shortDescription: The inlet temperature sensor has malfunctioned, affecting the dryer's ability to monitor incoming air temperature.
fullDescription: Error code tE3 indicates a failure in the inlet air thermistor, a sensor that monitors the temperature of air entering the dryer drum. This sensor helps the control board optimize drying performance by accounting for ambient air temperature. While not all Samsung dryer models include this third sensor, those that do use it to improve energy efficiency and drying accuracy. A failed inlet thermistor primarily affects the precision of sensor dry cycles.

causes.cause: Inlet thermistor failure due to age or contamination
Disconnected inlet thermistor connector
Lint contamination on the sensor affecting its readings
Wiring harness damage near the inlet area

symptoms: Error code tE3 displayed on the control panel
Sensor dry cycles are inaccurate (over or under drying)
Dryer may refuse to start certain cycle types
Energy efficiency decreases noticeably

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, restore power, and test with a timed dry cycle to see if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Locate and clean the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. The inlet thermistor is usually located near the air intake area. Remove lint or debris covering the sensor. Check the wire connection.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. Expect approximately 10,000 ohms at room temperature. Replace if out of specification.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting hardware, disconnect the old sensor, install the replacement, and reconnect the wiring harness.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests good and the connector is secure but the error persists, professional board-level diagnosis is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer tE3 inlet thermistor error

faq.q: Is the tE3 error serious enough to stop using the dryer?
faq.a: The dryer may still function on timed dry cycles, but sensor dry modes will be inaccurate. For best performance and safety, replace the sensor promptly.

faq.q: Can I bypass the inlet thermistor?
faq.a: Bypassing any temperature sensor is not recommended as it compromises safety features and can lead to overheating or fire risks.

faq.q: Are all three thermistors (tE1, tE2, tE3) the same part?
faq.a: In many Samsung models, all thermistors use the same part number (DC32-00007A) since they have the same resistance specification. However, confirm with your model's parts diagram before ordering.

title: Heater/Heating Error
shortDescription: The dryer's heating system has malfunctioned, with temperature exceeding safety limits or the heater not responding to control signals.
fullDescription: Error code hE on Samsung dryers indicates a heating system malfunction. This error is triggered when the control board detects the exhaust temperature has exceeded the safety threshold, typically above 220 degrees F. This usually means the dryer is overheating, which is most commonly caused by a clogged exhaust vent system restricting airflow. It can also be caused by a failed cycling thermostat, a stuck heater relay on the control board, or a shorted heating element that will not turn off. This is a critical safety error that should be addressed immediately due to fire risk.

causes.cause: Clogged or restricted dryer exhaust vent system
Failed cycling thermostat not regulating heater temperature
Stuck heater relay on the control board keeping heater always on
Shorted heating element that does not cycle off when commanded

symptoms: Error code hE on the display
Dryer exterior feels extremely hot to the touch
Burning smell coming from the dryer
Clothes come out scorched or extremely hot
Dryer shuts off mid-cycle for safety

fixSteps.title: Stop using the dryer immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If the hE error appears with a burning smell or excessive heat, stop the dryer immediately and unplug it. Allow it to cool completely before performing any inspection.

fixSteps.title: Clean the entire exhaust vent system
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dryer from the wall vent. Use a dryer vent cleaning kit (long flexible brush) to clean the entire vent duct from the dryer to the exterior wall outlet. Remove all lint buildup. Check the exterior vent flap for obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the lint trap housing
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint screen and use a narrow vacuum attachment to clean deep inside the lint trap housing. Lint can accumulate below the trap in the housing, restricting airflow even when the screen is clean.

fixSteps.title: Test the cycling thermostat and heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and access the heater assembly. Test the cycling thermostat for continuity (should be closed at room temperature). Test the heating element for continuity (should show 10-50 ohms). Also check for a ground short by testing between each element terminal and the element housing (should show no continuity).

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the vent does not resolve the issue and the thermostats and element test normal, the control board's heater relay may be stuck closed. This requires professional board repair or replacement. Also call a professional if you smell burning even after resolving the error.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer hE heater error overheating vent cleaning

faq.q: Is the hE error a fire hazard?
faq.a: The hE error itself is a safety feature that stops the dryer when overheating is detected. However, the condition that caused the overheating (usually a clogged vent) is a fire hazard. Clean the exhaust vent immediately and do not operate the dryer until the cause is resolved.

faq.q: How often should I clean my dryer vent?
faq.a: Samsung recommends cleaning the dryer exhaust vent at least once a year. If you dry loads frequently or notice longer drying times, clean it more often. The lint screen should be cleaned after every load.

faq.q: Can a clogged vent cause permanent damage?
faq.a: Yes. Restricted airflow causes the dryer to run hotter than designed, which can damage thermostats, the heating element, the control board, and even the drum bearings. In the worst case, it can cause a dryer fire.

title: Door Open Error
shortDescription: The dryer door is open or the door switch is not detecting a properly closed door.
fullDescription: Error code dC indicates the dryer's door switch does not detect the door as being closed. The dryer requires a confirmed door-closed signal before it will start or continue a drying cycle. This is a fundamental safety interlock. The error can be caused by a door that is not fully latched, a misaligned door catch, a faulty door switch, or a broken door hinge that prevents proper closure. On some models, this code may display as dF (door fail) or dC1/dC2.

causes.cause: Door not fully closed or latched
Faulty door switch not detecting door closure
Misaligned door catch or strike plate
Broken door hinge preventing proper closure

symptoms: Error code dC on the display
Dryer will not start when the start button is pressed
Dryer stops immediately after starting
Door does not click or feel secure when closed

fixSteps.title: Close the door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door fully and close it firmly until you hear a distinct click. Check that no clothing is caught in the door preventing full closure. Try starting the dryer.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch and strike
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door catch on the door and the strike plate on the dryer body. Look for damage, wear, or misalignment. If the strike is bent or the catch is worn, the door may not engage the switch properly. Realign or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Locate the door switch behind the front panel near the door opening. Disconnect the switch wires. Test for continuity with a multimeter: the switch should show continuity when pressed (door closed) and no continuity when released (door open). Replace if faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front panel of the dryer to access the door switch. The switch is usually held by one or two screws or a retaining clip. Disconnect the wires, remove the old switch, install the new one, and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the door switch, catch, and hinge all appear functional but the dC error persists, the wiring between the door switch and control board may be damaged, or the control board itself may have a fault.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer dC door error door switch replacement

faq.q: Can I bypass the door switch to test the dryer?
faq.a: It is not recommended to bypass the door switch. It is a critical safety device that prevents the dryer drum from spinning with the door open. A failed door switch is inexpensive and easy to replace.

faq.q: Why does my dryer door not stay closed?
faq.a: A worn door catch, stretched door hinge, or weakened door strike spring can prevent the door from staying latched. Inspect these components and replace any that show wear.

faq.q: Is the dC error the same as dF?
faq.a: Yes, on many Samsung models they are equivalent. The dC (door check) and dF (door fail) codes both indicate the door switch is not confirming the door is closed. Troubleshooting is the same.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: There is a communication failure between the main control board and the user interface board in the dryer.
fullDescription: Error code Et indicates an internal communication error between the dryer's main control board (located at the back of the dryer) and the user interface board (behind the control panel at the top). These boards exchange data through a serial communication bus. When communication is interrupted or corrupted, the Et error is triggered. This can result from a power surge, a loose ribbon cable, a failed board, or electromagnetic interference.

causes.cause: Loose or disconnected ribbon cable between the two boards
Power surge damaging communication circuits
Failed user interface (display) board
Failed main control board

symptoms: Error code Et displayed on the panel
Dryer does not respond to any button presses
Display shows garbled or incomplete information
Dryer powers on but cannot start any cycle

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes to allow both boards to fully reset. Restore power and check if the Et error clears.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the communication cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Remove the top panel and locate the ribbon cable connecting the user interface board to the main control board. Disconnect and firmly reconnect both ends. Ensure the connectors are fully seated.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the cable for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the ribbon cable or wiring harness for pinched areas, burn marks, or broken traces. Replace the cable if damage is found.

fixSteps.title: Replace the user interface board
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable is intact and the error persists, the user interface board is the more common failure point. Unplug the dryer, remove the control panel, disconnect the old board, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating the cable does not fix the Et error, professional diagnosis is recommended to determine which board has failed before purchasing expensive replacement parts.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer Et error communication board fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause the Et error?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage the communication circuits on either board. If the Et error appeared after a storm or power outage, a surge may have damaged one of the boards. Using a surge protector for the dryer circuit can help prevent this.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing the Et error or should I buy a new dryer?
faq.a: If the dryer is less than 7-8 years old, repairing the Et error is usually worthwhile. A board replacement costs $100-$250 for parts. Compare total repair cost to the price of a new dryer.

faq.q: Can I determine which board failed without replacing both?
faq.a: A technician with the Samsung diagnostic tool can sometimes determine which board has failed. Without special tools, the most cost-effective approach is to start by replacing the communication cable, then the user interface board (more common failure), and finally the main board if needed.

title: Frequency Error
shortDescription: The dryer has detected an abnormal power supply frequency, indicating an electrical supply issue.
fullDescription: Error code FC indicates the dryer's control board has detected that the incoming electrical power frequency is outside the acceptable range. In North America, the standard is 60 Hz. If the frequency deviates significantly, it can cause motors and electronic components to malfunction. This error is rare in normal household settings and is more common with generator power, unstable utility supply, or incorrect voltage connections. The dryer will not operate until stable power at the correct frequency is restored.

causes.cause: Running the dryer on a portable generator with unstable frequency
Unstable utility power supply with frequency fluctuations
Loose or corroded connections at the dryer's terminal block
Faulty main control board frequency detection circuit

symptoms: Error code FC on the display panel
Dryer will not start any cycle
Dryer makes unusual humming or buzzing sounds
Error appears intermittently, especially during peak electrical usage times

fixSteps.title: Check the power source
fixSteps.instruction: If the dryer is connected to a generator, switch to regular household power. Generators often produce unstable frequency that triggers the FC error. The dryer requires clean, stable 60 Hz power.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes and restore power. If the error was caused by a temporary frequency fluctuation, the reset should clear it.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the dryer's electrical connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Remove the terminal block cover at the back. Check that all wire connections are tight and there is no corrosion. Loose connections can cause voltage and frequency irregularities at the control board.

fixSteps.title: Test the wall outlet voltage and frequency
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter with frequency measurement capability to test the outlet. Electric dryers need 240V at 60 Hz (in North America). If the frequency reads significantly different from 60 Hz, contact your power utility.

whenToCallPro: If the power supply tests normal and the FC error persists after a reset, the control board's frequency detection circuit may be faulty. Call a technician for board diagnosis. Also consult an electrician if voltage or frequency issues are found at the outlet.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer FC frequency error fix

faq.q: Can I use my Samsung dryer with a generator?
faq.a: Samsung dryers require stable 60 Hz power. Many portable generators produce inconsistent frequency, which triggers the FC error. If you must use a generator, use an inverter generator that produces clean, stable power.

faq.q: Is the FC error dangerous?
faq.a: The FC error itself is not dangerous; it is a protective feature. However, unstable power supply can damage electronic components over time, so the underlying power issue should be resolved.

faq.q: Can a home UPS or power conditioner help?
faq.a: A power conditioner can help stabilize voltage and frequency, but dryers draw very high current (30+ amps), making it impractical and expensive to run through a standard UPS. An inverter generator or stable utility power is the practical solution.

title: Power Supply Voltage Error
shortDescription: The dryer is not receiving the correct voltage from the electrical supply, affecting normal operation.
fullDescription: Error code 9C1 indicates the dryer's control board has detected an abnormal voltage condition from the power supply. Samsung electric dryers require a 240V supply (in North America), and gas dryers require 120V. If the voltage drops below or exceeds the acceptable range, the 9C1 error is triggered. This can be caused by a tripped breaker on one leg of a 240V circuit, a faulty outlet, a damaged power cord, or a utility voltage problem. The 9E code on older models is equivalent.

causes.cause: One leg of the 240V circuit breaker has tripped (electric dryers)
Loose connection at the dryer terminal block or wall outlet
Damaged or faulty power cord
Utility voltage fluctuation or brown-out condition

symptoms: Error code 9C1 or 9E on the display
Dryer runs but produces no heat (getting only 120V instead of 240V)
Dryer will not start at all
Display flickers or dims intermittently

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Go to your home electrical panel. The dryer circuit is typically a double-pole 30-amp breaker. Check that both halves are fully in the ON position. If one has tripped, reset it by flipping it fully OFF then back ON.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the power cord and outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and examine the power cord for damage, scorch marks, or bent prongs. Check the wall outlet for burn marks or looseness. If the outlet feels loose or shows damage, have an electrician replace it.

fixSteps.title: Check the dryer terminal block
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and remove the terminal block cover on the back. Verify all wire connections are tight. Loose connections can cause voltage drops. Tighten all terminal screws securely.

fixSteps.title: Test the outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, measure voltage at the dryer outlet. You should read approximately 240V between the two hot terminals, and 120V between each hot terminal and the neutral. If readings are significantly off, the problem is in your home wiring or utility supply.

whenToCallPro: If the breaker, outlet, cord, and terminal block are all fine but the 9C1 error persists, call an electrician to test the home wiring and verify proper voltage at the dryer circuit. If the voltage is correct and the error continues, the control board may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dryer 9C1 voltage error fix

faq.q: Why does my electric dryer run but produce no heat?
faq.a: An electric dryer on a 240V circuit uses both legs of the circuit. If one breaker leg trips, the dryer receives only 120V, which is enough to run the motor and control panel but not enough to power the 240V heating element. This results in the 9C1 error and no heat.

faq.q: Is the 9C1 error the same as 9E?
faq.a: Yes. The 9E code is used on older Samsung dryer models, while 9C1 is the updated code on newer models. Both indicate a power supply voltage issue.

faq.q: Can a bad power cord cause the 9C1 error?
faq.a: Yes. A damaged power cord with a broken conductor on one leg will deliver only 120V instead of 240V. Inspect the cord for damage and replace if necessary. Always use a cord rated for the dryer's amperage.

For gas dryers, it may indicate the 120V supply is too low or too high. The dryer may partially operate (lights and display work) but cannot generate heat or may refuse to start entirely.

fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Examine the power cord along its entire length. Look for scorch marks, cracks, or damage. Check the plug prongs for corrosion or bending. Replace the cord if any damage is found.

fixSteps.title: Tighten terminal block connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the terminal block cover. Ensure all three or four wire connections are secure and the screws are tight. Loose terminals cause intermittent voltage drops.

fixSteps.title: Test outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure voltage at the wall outlet with a multimeter. Electric dryers should read 240V across hot terminals. If only 120V is present, a breaker or wiring issue exists in your home electrical system.

whenToCallPro: If the circuit breaker, outlet, and cord are all in good condition, call an electrician to check your home's dryer circuit wiring for faults.

faq.q: Can a power strip cause the 9E error?
faq.a: Samsung dryers should never be connected through power strips, extension cords, or surge protectors. They must be plugged directly into a dedicated outlet. Using a power strip can cause voltage drops that trigger the 9E error and also creates a fire hazard.

faq.q: Why does only one leg of my breaker trip?
faq.a: While double-pole breakers are designed so both poles trip together, internal mechanical issues or a partial short on one leg of the circuit can cause only one pole to trip. Have an electrician inspect the breaker and circuit.

faq.q: Is the 9E error the same on gas and electric dryers?
faq.a: The error code is the same, but the underlying cause differs. Electric dryers get 9E from a 240V supply issue (usually a tripped half-breaker), while gas dryers get 9E from a 120V supply issue (low voltage, loose connection, etc.).

title: High Temperature / Heater Cycling Error
shortDescription: The dryer has detected abnormally high temperatures, indicating a heater regulation failure or airflow restriction.
fullDescription: Error code HC signals that the dryer's internal temperature has exceeded safe operating limits. The control board monitors exhaust temperature continuously, and when it rises above the safety threshold (typically around 220 degrees F), the HC error is generated and the heater is shut off. This code is related to the hE error but may appear on different Samsung dryer models. The most common cause is restricted exhaust airflow from a clogged vent system. It can also indicate a failed thermostat or a heating element that will not cycle off.

Failed cycling thermostat not regulating heater temperature
Heating element grounded to its housing causing continuous heating

fixSteps.title: Stop the dryer and let it cool

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent system
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dryer from the wall vent duct. Use a long flexible dryer vent brush to clean the entire vent from the dryer to the outside. Also vacuum inside the dryer's lint trap housing and around the blower area.

fixSteps.title: Check the cycling thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Access the cycling thermostat (on the blower housing or heater box). Disconnect the wires and test for continuity. At room temperature, the thermostat should be closed (continuity present). If open at room temperature, it has failed and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect one wire from the heating element. Set your multimeter to ohms. Test between each element terminal and the element housing or ground. There should be no continuity (infinite resistance). If continuity is present, the element is grounded to the housing and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If vent cleaning and thermostat/element checks do not resolve the HC error, the control board relay may be stuck. A technician should test the board relay and replace if necessary. Always call a professional if there is evidence of burning or fire damage inside the dryer.

faq.q: What is the difference between HC, HC1, and HC2?
faq.a: HC is the general high-temperature error. HC1 typically refers to the first heater zone, and HC2 to the second heater zone on models with dual heater elements. Troubleshooting is similar for all three.

faq.q: How do I know if my vent is clogged?
faq.a: Signs of a clogged vent include: drying takes longer than normal, clothes are very hot at the end of a cycle, the outside of the dryer feels hot, humidity builds in the laundry room, and you can feel little to no airflow at the exterior vent hood.

faq.q: Should I switch from a 3-prong to 4-prong cord?
faq.a: Modern building codes require 4-prong dryer outlets, which include a separate neutral and ground. If you have an older 3-prong outlet, upgrading to a 4-prong setup is safer and can help prevent neutral-related issues.

While the dryer may still operate with the clg warning, drying times will be longer, energy consumption will increase, and there is an elevated fire risk from lint buildup in the vent.

causes.cause: Lint buildup in the exhaust vent duct
Crushed or kinked vent hose behind the dryer
Exterior vent flap stuck closed or blocked by debris
Excessively long or complex vent duct routing with too many bends

symptoms: clg or cg displayed on the panel
Clothes take longer than normal to dry
Dryer exterior feels hot during operation
Excessive humidity in the laundry room during dryer operation
Little or no airflow felt at the exterior vent hood

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint screen
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint screen and clean off all lint. If the screen has a waxy buildup from dryer sheets, wash it with warm soapy water and a soft brush, then dry completely before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint trap housing
fixSteps.instruction: With the lint screen removed, use a narrow vacuum attachment or a long lint trap brush to clean deep inside the lint trap housing where lint accumulates below the screen.

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent duct
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the vent duct from the back of the dryer. Use a long flexible dryer vent cleaning brush (available at hardware stores) to clean the entire duct from the dryer to the exterior wall. Also clean the duct from the exterior opening. Vacuum any loose lint.

fixSteps.title: Check the exterior vent hood
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and locate the dryer vent exit. Ensure the flap opens freely and is not blocked by lint, debris, snow, ice, or a bird nest. Clean the flap and surrounding area.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot access the full vent duct (such as vents that run through walls or attics), call a professional dryer vent cleaning service. Also consider professional vent rerouting if the current duct has excessive bends or length.

faq.q: Is the clg warning an error code?
faq.a: Not technically. The clg display is a maintenance warning from the Vent Sensor feature. It is alerting you that exhaust airflow is restricted and the vent needs cleaning. The dryer will still operate, but cleaning should be done promptly.

faq.q: How do I reset the clg warning after cleaning?
faq.a: After cleaning the vent, run a drying cycle. The clg warning should clear automatically once the Vent Sensor detects improved airflow. If it does not clear, try power cycling the dryer.

faq.q: How often should I clean my dryer vent?
faq.a: Samsung and fire safety experts recommend cleaning the dryer exhaust vent at least once per year. If you dry large loads frequently or notice longer drying times, clean it more often. Lint accumulation is the leading cause of dryer fires.

faq.a: No. The cg display is simply an alternate way the clg (clog) warning appears on certain Samsung dryer models. The meaning and solution are identical.

faq.q: Can I disable the Vent Sensor warning?
faq.a: The Vent Sensor warning cannot be permanently disabled as it is a safety feature. However, properly cleaning the vent will clear the warning. If the sensor itself is faulty, it can be replaced.

faq.q: What type of vent material is best for dryers?
faq.a: Rigid aluminum or rigid galvanized steel duct is recommended. Avoid vinyl or foil accordion-style duct, which traps lint in the ridges and is a fire hazard. Smooth-wall duct provides the best airflow.

title: AC Power Line Error (Display Variant)
shortDescription: A variant display of the AC power line error, indicating the dryer is not receiving proper electrical power.
fullDescription: The '1 AC' display is an alternate rendering of the AC power line error that appears on certain Samsung dryer models. The meaning is the same as the standard AC error: the control board has detected an anomaly in the incoming AC power supply. Troubleshooting follows the same steps as the standard AC error.

causes.cause: Voltage imbalance or instability on the power circuit

Partially tripped circuit breaker
Control board power input circuit failure

Display may flicker
Error appears intermittently

fixSteps.title: Reset the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dryer breaker fully and turn it back on after 30 seconds. Ensure both poles are engaged.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check all power connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the power cord, outlet, and terminal block for loose or corroded connections. Tighten everything.

fixSteps.title: Measure outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to verify 240V across the hot terminals and 120V from each hot to neutral.

whenToCallPro: If power at the outlet is confirmed correct but the error persists, call an appliance technician for control board diagnosis.
